title: Vanier Institute Conference Funding 2026 – Financial Support for Graduate Students and Postdoctoral Fellows in Family Science
summary: Are you a master s student, doctoral candidate, or postdoctoral fellow working in family science? The Vanier Institute of the Family Conference Funding 2026 program is now open for applications. This funding opportunity is designed to support graduate students and postdoctoral researchers who are actively engaged in family science knowledge exchange and wish to participate in academic conferences. If you are presenting research, sharing findings, or contributing to discussions that advance the understanding of families and social wellbeing, this program can help offset your participation costs. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); About the Vanier Institute of the Family The Vanier Institute of the Family is a Canadian organization dedicated to advancing knowledge and research about families. Through research dissemination and knowledge exchange initiatives, the Institute supports evidence-based dialogue that strengthens family wellbeing and policy development. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); This conference funding initiative reflects the Institute s commitment to supporting emerging scholars and researchers in the field of family science. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); What the Conference Funding Covers The funding supports participation in conferences related to family science knowledge exchange. While specific amounts and allowable expenses should be confirmed on the official page, support typically contributes toward: Conference registration fees Travel expenses Accommodation costs Knowledge dissemination activities Applicants should carefully review eligible expenses and funding limits before applying. Why You Should Apply Participating in academic conferences provides valuable opportunities to: Present your research to an international audience Expand your academic and professional network Receive feedback from experts in the field Strengthen your CV and academic profile Increase the visibility and impact of your research ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); This funding can reduce financial barriers and help you maximize these professional development opportunities. Who Is Eligible? The funding is available to: Master s students Doctoral students Postdoctoral fellows Applicants must be engaged in family science research and knowledge exchange activities. Application Timeline Applications for the 2026 cycle are now open. Applicants are strongly encouraged to review the official page for specific submission deadlines and funding timelines, as late applications may not be considered. Final Thoughts Conference participation plays a critical role in shaping academic careers, particularly for early-career researchers. If your work contributes to advancing family science, this funding opportunity can help you share your insights, strengthen collaborations, and contribute meaningfully to research-informed dialogue. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); How to Apply Interested applicants should review the detailed eligibility requirements and submit their application via the official link. For more OFY conferences opportunities, Click Here. title: 3rd Global MAB Youth Forum 2026 — Fully Funded UNESCO Youth Forum in Italy (Apply Now)
summary: Key Details Organizer: UNESCO Man and the Biosphere (MAB) Programme Location: Rosolina Mare, Italy Dates: 18 22 May 2026 Format: In-person Age range: 18 35 years Maximum participants: 200 Theme: Committed to the Future We Want Funding: Accommodation and meals covered; limited travel support available Application deadline: 23 March 2026 (23:59 CET) Contact: mab@unesco.org The third Global MAB Youth Forum marks a key milestone for strengthening youth participation in UNESCO s Man and the Biosphere (MAB) Programme. UNESCO 18 May 2026 22 May 2026 Theme Building on the 2017 and 2019 Global MAB Youth Forums, the third forum adopts the theme Committed to the Future We Want. Aligned with the Hangzhou Strategic Action Plan (2026 2035), it reflects MAB Youth s strengthened commitment to addressing biodiversity loss, climate change, and sustainable development. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Objectives The forum aims to: Enable MAB Youth to engage with and contribute to the implementation of the Hangzhou Strategic Action Plan (2026 2035), with particular attention to Action Target 20 on developing the MAB Youth Network. Translate findings from the World MAB Youth Mapping Survey into shared priorities, innovative approaches, and concrete pathways for action. Facilitate the exchange of youth-led practices and initiatives in Biosphere Reserves related to biodiversity conservation, sustainable development, and community well-being. Foster coordination, leadership, and capacity-building among youth actors across the World Network of Biosphere Reserves. Produce concrete youth-led outputs and recommendations that inform MAB governance and future action at national, regional, and global levels. Identify strategies to strengthen the integration of youth within MAB governance structures and decision-making. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Who can apply The Forum is intended for young people aged 18 35 who are actively engaged in or connected to UNESCO Biosphere Reserves (e.g., living, working, studying, volunteering, conducting research, or supporting BR-related activities). The maximum number of participants is 200. The Call for Expression of Interest will close on 23 March 2026 at 23:59 CET. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Two distinct application pathways are available: National MAB Youth Focal Points: Designated National MAB Youth Focal Points are invited to apply as official representatives within the framework of the MAB Programme. In view of their official national designation, National MAB Youth Focal Points who submit the required documentation shall be eligible to participate in the Forum without being subject to a competitive selection process. MAB Youth Participants (Open Call Other than National MAB Youth Focal Points): Eligible young people aged 18 35 who are actively engaged in or connected to UNESCO Biosphere Reserves (e.g., living, working, studying, volunteering, conducting research, or supporting BR-related activities) may apply through an open Expression of Interest process. Selection will be conducted competitively based on merit, geographical balance, gender parity, and relevance to the Forum s objectives. How to apply Applicants should submit an online expression of interest through the application form(s). The application should include basic information and, depending on the category, supporting documents. National MAB Youth Focal Points National MAB Youth Focal Points are requested to submit the following documents: A confirmation letter of designation issued by the MAB Secretariat, confirming their official appointment as National MAB Youth Focal Point. A short description of their experience, initiatives, or engagement related to UNESCO Biosphere Reserves, including their role and key outcomes. (Max. 250 words) A brief statement of contribution (max. 250 words) outlining what they can bring to the 3rd MAB Youth Forum in their role as National MAB Youth Focal Point. A brief statement (max. 250 words) explaining how they plan to follow up at national or regional levels after the Forum. An indication of whether they have confirmed financial support to cover their travel costs. If yes, supporting documentation may be uploaded. APPLY MAB Youth Participants (open call) Applicants are requested to submit the following: A recent Curriculum Vitae (CV). A short description of their experience, initiatives, or engagement related to UNESCO Biosphere Reserves, including their role and key outcomes. (Max. 250 words) A statement outlining the contributions they intend to bring to the Forum and the outcomes they hope to achieve. (Max. 250 words) A brief explanation of how they plan to apply the outcomes of the Forum in their Biosphere Reserve, community, or country after the event. (Max. 250 words) A letter of endorsement or support from a Biosphere Reserve or respective MAB National Committee or National Commission for UNESCO. An indication of whether they have confirmed financial support to cover their travel costs. If yes, supporting documentation may be uploaded. APPLY Travel and financial arrangements Food and accommodation costs in Italy during the Forum will generously be covered by the organizers. Limited financial support may be provided to assist participants with travel expenses, depending on funding availability and eligibility criteria. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Organisers Po Delta Biosphere Reserve Colli Euganei Biosphere Reserve Ministry of the Environment and Energy Security Ministry of Foreign Affairs and International Cooperation Fondazione Cassa di Risparmio di Padova e Rovigo Italian National Commission for UNESCO UNESCO MAB Secretariat UNESCO Regional Bureau for Science and Culture in Europe Veneto Region Contact UNESCO MAB Secretariat: mab@unesco.org Useful links Man and the Biosphere Programme UNESCO Venice Po Delta Biosphere Reserve Colli Euganei Biosphere Reserve Source for more info: https://www.unesco.org/en/articles/3rd-global-mab-youth-forum-2026-committed-future-we-want The post 3rd Global MAB Youth Forum 2026 — Fully Funded UNESCO Youth Forum in Italy (Apply Now) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply Now: €50,000 Funding for SMEs Developing Space-Enabled Environmental Solutions.
summary: The 1st Open Call for the Financial Support Program is now open, offering funding to small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) that want to leverage downstream space technologies to create environmentally sustainable and circular solutions. This initiative empowers companies to integrate Earth Observation (EO), remote sensing, and satellite navigation data into innovative processes, services, or products that reduce environmental impact. What the Program Offers This Financial Support Program provides funding for SMEs to contract External Service Providers who can help design, develop, or implement space-enabled green solutions . These solutions can either: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Improve the company s own internal operations Deliver environmental benefits to customers and end users ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Example Use Cases Optimizing internal processes to reduce waste, emissions, or resource consumption Developing products or services for environmental monitoring and resource management ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Projects can range from early-stage ideas to fully developed solutions . Funding Details Total Budget: 1,250,000 Number of SMEs Supported: 35 Maximum Funding per SME: 50,000 Who Can Apply? This Open Call is designed for SMEs that: Are based in EU Member States or Horizon Europe Associated Countries Can demonstrate potential to use downstream space data (e.g., EO, remote sensing, satellite navigation) to reduce environmental impact ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); SME Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the EU SME definition: Fewer than 250 employees Annual turnover not exceeding 50 million OR balance sheet total not exceeding 43 million ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Priority Areas The program gives special attention to solutions that focus on: Circularity, resource efficiency, and waste management Environmentally responsible management of raw materials Advanced environmental monitoring solutions Why Apply? This program offers SMEs a unique opportunity to: Access funding for innovation and sustainability Integrate cutting-edge space data technologies Improve environmental performance and competitiveness Build partnerships with specialized service providers ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); If your business is ready to harness space-enabled data for environmental impact , this is your opportunity to turn your idea into a funded, real-world solution. How to Apply Applying is straightforward and completed online through the OnePass platform : Register or log in on OnePass Complete your application Describe your solution and required services from external providers Submit your application Once submitted, your application will be evaluated, and selected applicants will be matched with external service providers to implement their projects. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Application Deadline 31 March 2026 at 23:59 CET (Brussels time) Timeline Overview January 2026: Open Call launch February 2026: First cut-off March 2026: Final application deadline April 2026: Evaluation and selection of first batch of winners Following months: Selection of second batch and project implementation Up to 6 months after signing: Project implementation period Final stage: Review and voucher payment Support and Guidance Applicants are encouraged to review the Guide for Applicants carefully before submitting their proposals. The guide explains eligibility, application steps, and evaluation criteria. For more details about this funding opportunities, click Here . Start your application today and contribute to a more sustainable, circular future. Discover more Funding Opportunities .
